title: Authorization, Tenancy, Object, Function, and Property Policy · Contract Lens: Tenant Query Enforcement
summary: Routes check a broad role but not object ownership, tenant filters are optional, update bodies bind protected fields, and denials leak which records exist. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts tenant query enforcement increment in deny-by-default resource authorization decision. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive tenant from the principal, carry it through repository interfaces, constrain every query and cache key, and test cross-tenant changed cases.
Load only enough identity or policy data to enforce ownership and object access without trusting a user-selected identifier.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Tenant Query Enforcement · Theory Tenant Query Enforcement
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 7746 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce tenant, object action, and property policy as one deny-by-default decision; preserve response ownership after a client abort; then make OpenAPI and runtime disagree on nullability and block the release. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Tenant Query Enforcement contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Predict Tenant Query Enforcement · Theory Tenant Query Enforcement.
